title: Garlicky Chewy German Potato Fritters
description: Crispy on the outside, tender and garlicky inside, these German potato fritters are made egg‑free and rely on cold water and potato starch for that signature chewiness.
instructions: Peel and grate the potatoes, then squeeze out as much liquid as possible with a clean towel.
In a large bowl, combine the grated potatoes, potato starch, flour, baking soda, salt, pepper, minced garlic, and chopped parsley.
Add the cold water gradually, mixing until a firm dough forms that can be held together without falling apart.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat; it should be hot but not smoking (around 175 °C).
Drop spoonfuls of the dough into the skillet, flattening them slightly to form small patties.
Fry each side for 3–4 minutes until golden brown and crispy, turning carefully with a spatula.
Transfer cooked fritters to a paper‑torn plate to drain excess oil.
Serve hot with a dollop of vegan sour cream or a squeeze of lemon for extra zing.
